key_insight: PICASA uses cross-attention on single-cell RNA-seq from multiple patients to separate shared cell-type signals from patient-specific cancer signatures.
clinical_relevance: This could help researchers identify tumor heterogeneity and patient-specific molecular effects that may inform biomarker discovery and precision oncology studies.
method: Cross-attention neural network learns transferable gene/feature embeddings across patients.
Models cell-type-common expression while isolating residual patient-specific signatures.
Highlights gene-gene interaction patterns and residuals that may reflect somatic changes such as CNVs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful decomposition in three cancer datasets—breast, ovarian, and lung—recovering cell-type commonality and patient-specific signatures.
watch_item: Results rely on the assumption that cell types are widely shared across patients, so performance may drop when tumor composition or cell states differ substantially.
paper_title: Decomposing patient heterogeneity of single-cell cancer data by cross-attention neural networks
